segments.text: Lesson 33.

translations.zh-Hans: 第33课。

segments.text: A Day to Remember.

translations.zh-Hans: 难忘的一天。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What incident began the series of traffic accidents?

translations.zh-Hans: 什么事件引发了接连发生的交通事故？

segments.text: We have all experienced days when everything goes wrong.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们都经历过事事不顺的日子。

segments.text: A day may begin well enough, but suddenly everything seems to get out of control.

translations.zh-Hans: 一天开始时也许还很顺利，但突然间一切似乎都失去了控制。

segments.text: What invariably happens is that a great number of things choose to go wrong at precisely the same moment.

translations.zh-Hans: 往往是一大堆事情偏偏选择在同一时刻出错。

segments.text: It is as if a single, unimportant event set up a chain of reactions.

translations.zh-Hans: 仿佛一件无关紧要的小事引发了一连串反应。

segments.text: Let us suppose that you are preparing a meal and keeping an eye on the baby at the same time.

translations.zh-Hans: 假设你一边准备饭菜，一边照看婴儿。

segments.text: The telephone rings, and this marks the prelude to an unforeseen series of catastrophes.

translations.zh-Hans: 电话响了，这便成了一连串意外灾难的序幕。

segments.text: While you are on the phone, the baby pulls the tablecloth off the table, smashing half your best crockery and cutting himself in the process.

translations.zh-Hans: 你打电话时，婴儿把桌布从桌上扯了下来，打碎了你一半最好的餐具，自己也被划伤了。

segments.text: You hang up hurriedly and attend to baby, crockery, etc.

translations.zh-Hans: 你匆忙挂断电话，去照料孩子、收拾餐具等等。

segments.text: Meanwhile, the meal gets burnt.

translations.zh-Hans: 与此同时，饭烧糊了。

segments.text: As if this were not enough to reduce you to tears, your husband arrives, unexpectedly bringing three guests to dinner.

translations.zh-Hans: 仿佛这些还不足以让你掉泪，丈夫又突然带着三位客人回来吃晚饭。

segments.text: Things can go wrong on a big scale, a number of people recently discovered in Parramatta, a suburb of Sydney.

translations.zh-Hans: 事情也可能闹得很大，悉尼郊区帕拉马塔的一些人最近就有此体会。

segments.text: During the rush hour one evening, two cars collided, and both drivers began to argue.

translations.zh-Hans: 一天傍晚交通高峰时，两辆汽车相撞，双方司机争吵起来。

segments.text: The woman immediately behind the two cars happened to be a learner.

translations.zh-Hans: 紧跟在两辆车后面的女司机碰巧是个新手。

segments.text: She suddenly got into a panic and stopped her car.

translations.zh-Hans: 她突然惊慌失措，把车停了下来。

segments.text: This made the driver following her break hard.

translations.zh-Hans: 这使跟在她后面的司机猛踩刹车。

segments.text: His wife was sitting beside him holding a large cake.

translations.zh-Hans: 他的妻子坐在旁边，手里捧着一只大蛋糕。

segments.text: As she was thrown forward, the cake went right through the windscreen and landed on the road.

translations.zh-Hans: 她被甩向前方，蛋糕穿过挡风玻璃，落到了路上。

segments.text: Seeing a cake flying through the air, a lorry driver, who was drawing up alongside the car, pulled up all of a sudden.

translations.zh-Hans: 一名卡车司机正把车开到轿车旁边，看到蛋糕从空中飞过，便猛然停车。

segments.text: The lorry was loaded with empty beer bottles, and hundreds of them slid off the back of the vehicle and onto the road.

translations.zh-Hans: 卡车装满空啤酒瓶，数百只瓶子从车后滑落到公路上。

segments.text: This led to yet another angry argument.

translations.zh-Hans: 这又引发了一场激烈的争吵。

segments.text: Meanwhile, the traffic piled up behind.

translations.zh-Hans: 与此同时，后面的车辆越堵越多。

segments.text: It took the police nearly an hour to get the traffic on the move again.

translations.zh-Hans: 警察花了将近一个小时才使交通重新流动起来。

segments.text: In the meantime, the lorry driver had to sweep up hundreds of broken bottles.

translations.zh-Hans: 在此期间，卡车司机不得不清扫数百只破瓶子。

segments.text: Only two stray dogs benefited from all this confusion, for they greedily devoured what was left of the cake.

translations.zh-Hans: 这场混乱中只有两条流浪狗得了好处，它们贪婪地吃光了剩下的蛋糕。

segments.text: It was just one of those days.

translations.zh-Hans: 这真是那种倒霉透顶的日子。
